build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: Rooms have interactive hotspots that reveal objects, clues, or hidden compartments when clicked. Examining hotspots sometimes adds items to an inventory bar. At least four hotspots per room exist. Score 0 if there are no interactive hotspots, if clicking does nothing, or if no inventory exists. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Items can be combined with each other or used on hotspots to solve puzzles (key on lock, cipher on coded message, tool on mechanism). Using the correct item advances the puzzle sequence. Score 0 if items cannot be used, if combinations do not exist, or if puzzles have no item interaction. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Puzzles gate progress: solving one reveals the next. Each room has a sequence of at least three puzzles that must be solved in order to escape. Puzzle types include pattern matching, code entry, or logic deduction. Score 0 if there are no puzzles, if puzzles do not gate progress, or if fewer than two puzzles exist per room. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Narrative choices appear between puzzle segments that affect the story branch. Different choices lead to different rooms or endings. The player's choices are tracked. Score 0 if there are no narrative choices, if choices have no effect, or if the story is completely linear. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> room entry -> explore/puzzle/choose -> escape or fail -> result screen showing ending reached. Multiple endings exist (true ending and incomplete endings). Score 0 if any transition is missing or if only one ending exists. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least three rooms exist in sequence, each with a distinct visual theme and puzzle set. Later rooms are harder than earlier ones. Score 0 if only one room exists or if rooms are identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Puzzle types vary: at least three different puzzle mechanics are used across rooms (code entry, pattern matching, item combination, hidden object, logic grid). Score 0 if all puzzles use the same mechanic. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least five inventory items exist across the game with different sprites and uses. Items are not interchangeable — each has a specific puzzle it solves. Score 0 if fewer than three items exist or if items are generic. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Narrative branching creates at least two distinct paths through the game with different rooms or puzzle orders. The true ending requires specific choices. Score 0 if the game is completely linear with no branching. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The result screen shows escape time, puzzles solved, ending reached, and hints about paths not taken. Different endings have different result text and imagery. Score 0 if results are generic or if ending differences are not communicated. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with atmospheric suspense (dim lighting, locked door imagery). Score 0 if it launches into raw gameplay or default UI. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title -> room -> explore/puzzle/narrative -> next room or ending without restarting. The player always knows what items they have and what can be interacted with. Score 0 if transitions are broken or if inventory is inaccessible. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Room hotspots, inventory items, puzzle interfaces, and narrative choices are readable at 1280x720 without overlap. Interactive elements are distinguishable from background. Score 0 if the player cannot tell what is interactive. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Key moments have visible feedback: finding items shows a discovery effect, solving puzzles shows unlock animations, wrong attempts show failure cues, and narrative choices show consequence previews or transitions. Score 0 if important events have no visual feedback. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: rooms, items, puzzles, UI, and effects belong to a consistent atmospheric-suspense visual language with dim, moody tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Room backgrounds are real illustrated environments with detailed objects, furniture, and atmospheric lighting. Each room has a distinct visual identity. Score 0 if rooms are flat colored backgrounds. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Inventory items and puzzle interfaces are real illustrated objects with detail and personality. Items look like physical objects, not abstract icons. Score 0 if items are plain shapes or generic icons. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

UI uses styled suspense chrome: inventory bar has themed frames, puzzle interfaces have room-appropriate styling, narrative choices have dramatic presentation, and transitions use atmospheric effects. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
